message:
feat: buffer-reusing vectorized scan, group-commit flusher vacancy, and transaction API migration

Vectorized scan (fsqlite-vdbe):
- Replace per-batch Vec<u8> payload allocation with a reusable payload_buf
  that is cleared and refilled each iteration, eliminating allocator pressure
  on hot scan paths.
- Replace per-row parse_record() (which returns a freshly allocated Vec) with
  parse_record_into() that decodes into pre-allocated row_buffers, letting the
  scan amortize row-vector allocation across batches rather than per-row.
- Inline the predicate_matches() helper directly at the call site and clone
  the predicate Arc before the loop so the borrow checker does not force a
  reborrow of &mut self on every iteration.
- Add scan_reuses_decode_scratch_and_avoids_full_record_parse_calls test that
  asserts buffer pointer stability across batches and verifies that the record
  profile reports zero parse_record calls and exactly N parse_record_into
  calls, proving the allocation-free decode path is taken.

Group-commit consolidator (fsqlite-wal):
- Track promoted_epoch_flusher_vacant flag so that when a flush completes and
  pipelined batches are promoted back to FILLING, the system knows the
  promoted epoch has no explicitly claimed flusher.
- Add has_flusher_vacancy() query and claim_flusher_vacancy() mutator so a
  fresh submitter can take over the flusher role when the original flusher
  stopped after complete_flush() without calling begin_flush() again.
- Update all state transitions (submit, begin_flush, complete_flush,
  abort_flush, transition_to_filling) to maintain the vacancy flag correctly.
- Fix test_consolidator_basic to expect pipelined submissions during FLUSHING
  to succeed as Waiter (not error), and to expect FILLING (not COMPLETE)
  after complete_flush when pipelined batches exist.
- Fix test_consolidator_abort_flush to expect completed_epoch==0 after
  aborting the first epoch (epoch was never fully completed).
- Add test_consolidator_promoted_epoch_exposes_flusher_takeover_claim test
  covering the full promoted-epoch → vacancy → claim → re-flush lifecycle.

Transaction API migration (fsqlite, fsqlite-e2e):
- Replace raw conn.execute("BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK") calls with typed
  begin_transaction(), commit_transaction(), rollback_transaction() methods
  throughout the compat Transaction wrapper and the e2e executor, including
  batch execution, rollback recovery, and PreparedOpExecutor dispatch.
- This avoids SQL re-parsing overhead for transaction control and makes the
  intent explicit at the type level.

Pager (fsqlite-pager):
- Add published_visible_commit_seq_hint() default method to TransactionHandle
  trait so upper layers can query a transaction's own snapshot visibility
  boundary without re-binding against the global published plane.
- Rustfmt reformatting of match arms in refresh_header_state and a test
  assertion.

VFS (fsqlite-vfs):
- Rustfmt reformatting of posix_lock_with_timeout call sites in shared and
  pending lock acquisition paths.

Btree (fsqlite-btree):
- Rustfmt reformatting of write_overflow_chain call in payload assembly.

Connection (fsqlite-core):
- Rustfmt reformatting of rollback_transaction, reload_memdb_from_txn, and
  several pager_routing_tests for line-length compliance.

Beads metadata:
- Update .beads/issues.jsonl with refreshed issue tracking entries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Define the explicit exit criteria for claiming meaningful progress toward leapfrogging SQLite. This bead makes the success condition concrete instead of relying on whatever numbers happen to look good after a few optimizations land, and it has to distinguish transferable wins from one-profile lab wins.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The scorecard includes concrete target ratios or directional goals for c1, c4, and c8 benchmark cells.
- Retry, CPU-utilization, p50/p95/p99 latency-protection, responsiveness, and topology-stability requirements are part of the gate.
- The gate states what must hold on the recommended placement profile and what no-catastrophic-regression bounds must still hold on baseline or adversarial profiles.
- The gate is usable by final reporting without reinterpretation.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The scorecard includes concrete target ratios or directional goals for `c1`, `c4`, and `c8` benchmark cells.

NOTE: The original consolidated spec extract for this rollup lives in the bead comments.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T05:16:39.108097323Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T17:35:14.952857972Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T06:39:48.352457310Z","close_reason":"DUPLICATE: Superseded by finer-grained bd-7pxb (§12.10-12.12) + bd-1mrj (§12.13-12.14) + bd-16ov (§12.15-12.16) + bd-cfj0 (§12.17)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vr4u","depends_on_id":"bd-1x2z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vr4u","depends_on_id":"bd-257u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vr4u","depends_on_id":"bd-31t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2042,"issue_id":"bd-vr4u","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## §12.10-12.17 Transaction Control + ATTACH + EXPLAIN + VACUUM + PRAGMA + Expressions + Type Affinity + Time Travel

### Transaction Control (§12.10)
BEGIN [DEFERRED|IMMEDIATE|EXCLUSIVE|CONCURRENT]; COMMIT; END (=COMMIT); ROLLBACK.
SAVEPOINT name; RELEASE [SAVEPOINT] name; ROLLBACK TO [SAVEPOINT] name.

**Transaction modes:** DEFERRED (default, no locks until first read/write). IMMEDIATE (RESERVED lock immediately). EXCLUSIVE (EXCLUSIVE lock immediately; equivalent to IMMEDIATE in WAL mode). CONCURRENT (FrankenSQLite extension: MVCC concurrent writer mode with SI, conflicts on same page → SQLITE_BUSY_SNAPSHOT).

**Savepoints:** Stack-based. RELEASE X commits work since SAVEPOINT X and pops X + all more recent. ROLLBACK TO X undoes work since SAVEPOINT X but leaves X on stack.

### ATTACH/DETACH (§12.11)
`ATTACH expr AS schema-name; DETACH schema-name`. Main = "main", temp = "temp". Max 10 attached (SQLITE_MAX_ATTACHED). Cross-database transactions atomic only in rollback journal mode (standard SQLite); FrankenSQLite MUST support cross-database atomic WAL transactions via 2PC across WAL files.

### EXPLAIN (§12.12)
`EXPLAIN stmt` → VDBE bytecode (addr, opcode, p1-p5, comment).
`EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN stmt` → high-level plan (id, parent, notused, detail). Tree via id/parent.

### VACUUM (§12.13)
`VACUUM [schema]; VACUUM [schema] INTO filename`. Rebuilds database: create new, copy all, replace original. INTO = compact backup without modifying original.

### Other Statements (§12.14)
REINDEX [collation|table-or-index]. ANALYZE [schema|table-or-index] → sqlite_stat1/stat4.
PRAGMA [schema.]name [= value | (value)].

### Expression Syntax (§12.15)
Pratt parser (normative precedence in §10.2). Key: `NOT x = y` → `NOT (x = y)`. ESCAPE parsed as LIKE suffix. Unary binds tighter than COLLATE.

**Special forms:** CAST, CASE, EXISTS, IN, BETWEEN, COLLATE, LIKE/GLOB with ESCAPE, RAISE (trigger-only), JSON -> and ->> operators.

### Type Affinity Rules (§12.16)
Five affinities: TEXT, NUMERIC, INTEGER, REAL, BLOB.

**Comparison affinity rules:** (1) If one operand has INT/REAL/NUMERIC and other has TEXT/BLOB → apply numeric to TEXT/BLOB. (2) If one TEXT and other BLOB (no numeric) → apply TEXT to BLOB. (3) Same class or both BLOB → no conversion.

**Key distinction:** Affinity applied to operand needing conversion, not both. If both share class, no coercion.

### Time Travel Queries — Native Mode Extension (§12.17)
`SELECT ... FROM table FOR SYSTEM_TIME AS OF 'timestamp'` or `AS OF COMMITSEQ N`.

**Semantics:** (1) Determine target_commit_seq (binary search markers for timestamp, or direct for COMMITSEQ). (2) Create synthetic read-only snapshot S with S.high = target_commit_seq. (3) Execute query using normal MVCC resolution.

**Restrictions:** Time travel is read-only (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/DDL → SQLITE_ERROR). History pruned → explicit error. Tiered storage: fetch symbols on demand under Cx budgets.

 {"id":"bd-wba3f","title":"WASM 1.2: Create wasm-compatible sync primitive polyfills (Mutex, RwLock, Once, thread_id)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:15:44.594818509Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-07T21:11:15.318738Z","closed_at":"2026-03-07T21:11:15.318677Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-wba3f","depends_on_id":"bd-1dzd2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-wba3f","depends_on_id":"bd-1xmfa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2043,"issue_id":"bd-wba3f","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# Task 1.2: Wasm-Compatible Sync Primitive Polyfills

## What
Create a shared module providing single-threaded sync primitives for wasm32 targets. On native targets, these re-export `parking_lot` types. On wasm32, they provide lightweight single-threaded implementations.

## Why
`parking_lot` depends on OS-specific mutex primitives that don't exist in wasm32. Since browser WASM runs single-threaded within a Worker, we can use trivial single-threaded implementations (RefCell-based or no-op).

## Detailed Design

### Module Location
Create `crates/fsqlite-types/src/sync_primitives.rs` (since fsqlite-types is the foundation crate imported by all others).

### API Surface
```rust
// Re-exports that work on all platforms
pub use crate::sync_primitives::{Mutex, MutexGuard, RwLock, RwLockReadGuard, RwLockWriteGuard, Once, OnceCell};

#[cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))]
mod native {
    pub use parking_lot::{Mutex, MutexGuard, RwLock, RwLockReadGuard, RwLockWriteGuard, Once};
    // Also re-export from std where needed
}

#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
mod wasm {
    use std::cell::{RefCell, Ref, RefMut};

    pub struct Mutex<T>(RefCell<T>);
    impl<T> Mutex<T> {
        pub fn new(val: T) -> Self { Self(RefCell::new(val)) }
        pub fn lock(&self) -> MutexGuard<'_, T> { MutexGuard(self.0.borrow_mut()) }
        pub fn try_lock(&self) -> Option<MutexGuard<'_, T>> {
            self.0.try_borrow_mut().ok().map(MutexGuard)
        }
    }
    pub struct MutexGuard<'a, T>(RefMut<'a, T>);
    // ... Deref/DerefMut impls

    pub struct RwLock<T>(RefCell<T>);
    impl<T> RwLock<T> {
        pub fn new(val: T) -> Self { Self(RefCell::new(val)) }
        pub fn read(&self) -> RwLockReadGuard<'_, T> { RwLockReadGuard(self.0.borrow()) }
        pub fn write(&self) -> RwLockWriteGuard<'_, T> { RwLockWriteGuard(self.0.borrow_mut()) }
    }
    // ... etc.
}
```

### Thread ID Polyfill
```rust
#[cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))]
pub fn current_thread_id() -> u64 { std::thread::current().id().as_u64().get() }

#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
pub fn current_thread_id() -> u64 { 0 } // Single-threaded, always thread 0
```

### Time Polyfill
```rust
#[cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))]
pub fn now_ms() -> f64 { std::time::SystemTime::now().duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH).unwrap().as_millis() as f64 }

#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
pub fn now_ms() -> f64 { js_sys::Date::now() }
```

### Crossbeam-Epoch Polyfill
For wasm32, epoch-based reclamation is unnecessary (single-threaded = no concurrent readers). Provide a no-op implementation:
```rust
#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
pub mod epoch {
    pub struct Guard;
    pub fn pin() -> Guard { Guard }
    // Atomic<T> wraps a simple Cell<T>
}
```

### Key Constraints
- Must be `Send + Sync` on native targets (parking_lot provides this)
- On wasm32, `Send + Sync` bounds are less strict but some trait bounds may need `#[cfg]` adjustments
- The polyfill must have identical API surface to avoid `#[cfg]` at every call site
- Performance on native must not regress (zero-cost re-export)

### Acceptance Criteria
- Module compiles on both native and wasm32 targets
- All existing code that uses parking_lot can switch to `use fsqlite_types::sync::{Mutex, RwLock}`
- Zero performance regression on native (verified by existing benchmarks)
- `cargo check --target wasm32-unknown-unknown -p fsqlite-types` passes
","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:16:05Z"},{"id":2044,"issue_id":"bd-wba3f","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## CORRECTIONS (found during deep audit, 2026-03-02)

### Issue 1: Condvar polyfill missing
fsqlite-pager/src/arc_cache.rs:28 uses `parking_lot::{Condvar, Mutex}`. The polyfill must also include Condvar:
```rust
#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
pub struct Condvar;

#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
impl Condvar {
    pub fn new() -> Self { Self }
    pub fn wait<T>(&self, _guard: &mut MutexGuard<'_, T>) {
        // No-op on wasm32 (single-threaded — nothing to wait for)
        // In practice, code paths using Condvar should be gated out
    }
    pub fn notify_one(&self) { /* no-op */ }
    pub fn notify_all(&self) { /* no-op */ }
}
```

### Issue 2: fsqlite-types will need a new dependency on parking_lot
Currently fsqlite-types does NOT depend on parking_lot. Since the polyfill lives in fsqlite-types, adding:
```toml
[target.'cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))'.dependencies]
parking_lot = { workspace = true }
```
This changes the dependency graph — all crates that depend on fsqlite-types will transitively get parking_lot on native. This is acceptable since most already depend on it directly.

### Issue 3: web_time or js_sys dependency for time polyfill
The time polyfill using `js_sys::Date::now()` requires js-sys as a wasm32 dependency:
```toml
[target.'cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")'.dependencies]
js-sys = "0.3"
```
Alternative: use the `web-time` crate which provides a drop-in replacement for std::time that works on wasm32:
```toml
[target.'cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")'.dependencies]
web-time = "1.1"
```
The `web-time` approach is simpler because it provides `Instant` and `Duration` with the same API as std::time.

### Issue 4: getrandom/js propagation
The polyfill module should document that downstream crates using `rand`, `argon2`, or any crate that transitively depends on `getrandom` MUST add `getrandom = { version = "0.2", features = ["js"] }` to their wasm feature dependencies. This is a well-known wasm32 gotcha.

 {"id":"bd-wqgm","title":"RealDB Corpus: discovery/import policy + golden fixtures + manifest","description":"## Goal

Make the real-database corpus under `sample_sqlite_db_files/` safe, reproducible, self-describing, and scalable.

This sub-epic is about **inputs**: which DBs we use, how we snapshot them safely, how we track provenance/tags, and how we verify golden bytes never change.

## Key Outputs

- A clear **fixture inclusion policy** (size limits, PII/secrets handling, allowed roots, tagging taxonomy).
- Robust discovery (`corpus scan`) with predictable filtering + stable output.
- Robust import (`corpus import`) that always uses a consistent snapshot (SQLite backup API) and never touches originals.
- Manifest + checksums + metadata that are:
  - deterministic
  - validated by tests
  - sufficient to rerun benches/demos without tribal knowledge

## Safety Invariants

- Never mutate `/dp/...` originals.
- Never write to `golden/` except via explicit import flow.
- Never commit DB bytes from `/dp` to git (only `checksums.sha256`, `metadata/*.json`, `manifests/*.json`, docs).

## Acceptance

- `cargo run -p fsqlite-e2e --bin realdb-e2e -- corpus scan` finds candidates without false positives (non-sqlite files) and without recursion explosions.
- `corpus import` always produces a golden copy that passes `PRAGMA integrity_check`.
- `corpus verify` detects drift and reports actionable diagnostics.
- Tests enforce manifest/checksum consistency.","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance Criteria

1. Safety invariants are enforced by code paths in `crates/fsqlite-e2e` (not just docs):
   - No command ever writes to `/dp/...` originals.
   - All write workloads/corruption run only on per-run working copies.
   - Golden fixtures are treated as immutable; attempts to overwrite must fail loudly.
   - DB bytes (`*.db`, `*-wal`, `*-shm`, etc) remain git-ignored; only manifest/metadata/checksums are tracked.

2. Corpus lifecycle is usable end-to-end via CLI + docs:
   - Discover candidates (read-only): `realdb-e2e corpus scan ...`.
   - Import snapshot safely: `realdb-e2e corpus import ...` uses SQLite backup semantics and runs `PRAGMA integrity_check` on the captured golden.
   - Verify provenance: `realdb-e2e corpus verify ...` checks sha256/integrity and emits actionable diagnostics + stable exit codes.

3. Corpus bookkeeping is deterministic and mutually consistent:
   - `sample_sqlite_db_files/checksums.sha256` matches `golden/*`.
   - `sample_sqlite_db_files/metadata/*.json` exists for every fixture and contains provenance (source path, tags, capture time) without row contents.
   - `sample_sqlite_db_files/manifests/manifest.v1.json` is generated deterministically (sorted by db_id) and schema-valid.

4. Tests exist (unit + integration) to prevent regressions:
   - Schema-validates the manifest.
   - Detects checksum drift/missing metadata.
   - Ensures scan/import/verify never open/write `/dp` directly.

5. Developer experience: `crates/fsqlite-e2e/RUNBOOK.md` and `sample_sqlite_db_files/FIXTURES.md` provide copy-paste commands for:
   - adding a new fixture
   - verifying corpus health
   - running a smoke demo against fixtures","notes":"Notes / Design Constraints

- This epic is the corpus foundation for the RealDB mega-demos: correctness/perf/corruption all depend on fixtures being safe, reproducible, and provenance-checked.
- Prefer importing via a *backup API* (equivalent of `sqlite3 SRC ".backup 'DST'"`) rather than raw filesystem copies, because WAL-mode sources may be mid-write.
- Keep metadata "safe by construction": record schema/PRAGMAs/stats, but never row contents.
- Determinism matters: stable ordering in JSON outputs, stable db_id resolution, stable manifest generation.
- Reference implementation/docs live in:
  - `sample_sqlite_db_files/FIXTURES.md`
  - `sample_sqlite_db_files/manifests/manifest.v1.schema.json`
  - `crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/bin/profile_db.rs` (manifest/metadata generation)
  - `crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/bin/realdb_e2e.rs` (scan/import/verify commands)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-10T17:23:31.349899836Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T17:50:39.722656852Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T17:47:36.799875282Z","close_reason":"Duplicate plan epic: consolidated into bd-2als.* (canonical RealDB mega-demos epic).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-wqgm","depends_on_id":"bd-1n0i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-wqgm","depends_on_id":"bd-3giw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-wqgm","depends_on_id":"bd-5ic3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-wqgm","depends_on_id":"bd-i28l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-wqgm","depends_on_id":"bd-jwuo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-wqgm","depends_on_id":"bd-qaef","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

-{"id":"bd-zna34","title":"Persistent benchmark commit-path Bad file descriptor in FrankenSQLite","description":"## Goal
Restore the synthetic persistent Criterion benchmark as a trustworthy FrankenSQLite-vs-SQLite signal by fixing the current FrankenSQLite commit-path failure.

## Evidence
- On 2026-03-21 local Criterion runs, SQLite 2-thread completed, but FrankenSQLite crashed during the 2-thread group before producing a stable estimate.

Verified with full Criterion benchmark (14+ iterations, 0 errors) and integration test.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2504,"issue_id":"bd-zna34","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Root Cause Identified — IronVault (claude-opus-4-6)

### Diagnostic Evidence
Instrumented UnixFile::write() caught the exact failure:
```
WRITE_FAILED fd=5 arc_strong=3 closed=false path=…/-wal offset=16512
```

### Key Facts
1. The failing fd is 5 (the InodeInfo canonical fd for the WAL file)
2. Arc strong count is 3 (InodeInfo + 2 worker connections)  
3. UnixFile.closed is false — close() was NOT called
4. write_at(fd=5, offset=16512) returns EBADF despite the Arc being alive

### Diagnosis
The fd was invalidated at the OS level despite the Rust Arc<File> still holding it.
Bug manifests after Criterion iterations of setup/teardown. Most likely cause:
asupersync io_uring backend fd lifecycle issue (H3 from fault matrix).

### Next Steps
1. Test with io_uring disabled to confirm H3
2. Add proper io_uring op drain before fd close
3. Add fd validity guard in WAL write path
","created_at":"2026-03-22T16:30:21Z"},{"id":2505,"issue_id":"bd-zna34","author":"ubuntu","text":"## ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED AND FIXED — IronVault (claude-opus-4-6)

### Root Cause
**The WAL file was opened READONLY and the canonical fd was shared to writers.**

`install_existing_wal_backend_with_vfs()` (connection.rs:1174) and
`wal_file_present_with_vfs()` (connection.rs:1205) both opened the WAL file
with `VfsOpenFlags::READONLY | VfsOpenFlags::WAL`. On the first open for an
inode, the global `InodeInfo` table installs this READONLY fd as the canonical
fd. All subsequent connections that clone this fd via the fast path inherit
the read-only fd.

When the WAL group commit flusher calls `file.write_at()`, the `pwrite()`
syscall on a read-only fd returns `EBADF` on Linux. The fd is valid
(fcntl F_GETFD returns 1, /proc/self/fd exists), but writes are forbidden
because the fd was opened O_RDONLY.

### Diagnostic Evidence
```
[bd-zna34] WRITE EBADF DIAGNOSTICS:
fd=6 closed=false path=/tmp/.tmpSBeP7K-wal
fcntl(F_GETFD)=1 /proc/self/fd exists=true
Arc<File> strong=3 Arc<InodeInfo> strong=3
```
The fd is VALID but READONLY → pwrite returns EBADF.

### Fix (2 changes in connection.rs)
1. `install_existing_wal_backend_with_vfs`: Changed from READONLY-first to
   READWRITE-only. The READONLY fallback was defensive but poisoned the
   InodeInfo canonical fd.
2. `wal_file_present_with_vfs`: Changed probe to open READWRITE first,
   falling back to READONLY only if READWRITE fails (e.g., read-only FS).

### Verification
Ran the full Criterion 2-thread persistent benchmark 14+ iterations with
zero EBADF errors. Previously failed consistently within 3-5 iterations.

### Non-regression
- Concurrent writer mode: UNCHANGED (ON by default)
- No writer serialization introduced
- WAL append/sync path: UNCHANGED
- Fix is in connection setup, not in hot path

- CommitIndex: maps page numbers to latest commit_seq

- Conflict detection pipeline: (1) acquire exclusive page locks for write_set, (2) CommitIndex check: for each page, if CommitIndex[pgno] > T.begin_seq then conflict, (3) attempt deterministic rebase (section 5.10) if conflict pages overlap, (4) SSI validation (section 5.7.3) even if rebase succeeds, (5) commit on success or SQLITE_BUSY_SNAPSHOT on failure
- Page lock operations: try_acquire (vacant->insert, occupied->check same txn or SQLITE_BUSY), release (remove entry, panic if not held), release_all (iterate per-txn set, may group by shard)

### Spec Content (Lines 9168-9195)
First-committer-wins: When T2 attempts to commit and discovers T1 already committed modifications to the same page(s), T2 MUST detect this via CommitIndex lookup. The response depends on whether deterministic rebase (§5.10) can resolve the conflict.

Conflict detection pipeline:
1. Lock acquisition: T2 acquires exclusive page locks for its write_set
2. CommitIndex check: For each page in write_set, if CommitIndex[pgno] > T2.begin_seq → conflict
3. Rebase attempt: If conflict pages overlap, attempt deterministic rebase (§5.10)
4. SSI validation: Even if rebase succeeds, still run SSI check (§5.7.3)
5. Resolution: Commit on success, SQLITE_BUSY_SNAPSHOT on failure

### Unit Tests Required
1. test_first_committer_wins: T1 commits first, T2 detects conflict
2. test_no_conflict_different_pages: T1 and T2 touch different pages → both commit
3. test_conflict_with_successful_rebase: Same page but commuting ops → both commit
4. test_conflict_response_sqlite_busy: Non-rebasable conflict → SQLITE_BUSY_SNAPSHOT
5. test_commit_index_lookup_correctness: CommitIndex correctly tracks latest commit per page
","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:02:22Z"},{"id":2080,"issue_id":"bd-zppf","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECTION: §5.8 (spec lines ~8981-9167)

PURPOSE: Implement page lock table, FCW commit validation, and serialized/concurrent mode interaction.

## Page Lock Table Implementation (normative)

### Concurrent Mode (cross-process)
- SharedPageLockTable in foo.db.fsqlite-shm (§5.6.3) is THE canonical lock table
- ALL page-level writer exclusion MUST be enforced via shared-memory table, NOT in-process HashMap

### Normal commit/abort (fast path)
- Release page locks by iterating in-process page_locks set (touch only locked pages)

### Crash cleanup (slow path)
- MUST use shared-memory scan release_page_locks_for(txn_id) (§5.6.3) -- crashed process has no in-process set

## Single-Process Reference Implementation (NOT cross-process safe)
- 64-shard InProcessPageLockTable (parking_lot::Mutex<HashMap<PageNumber, TxnId>>)
- Shard selection: pgno.get() as usize & (LOCK_TABLE_SHARDS - 1)
- try_acquire: vacant→insert, occupied→check same txn (idempotent) or SQLITE_BUSY
- release: remove entry, panic if not held by txn
- release_all: iterate per-txn lock set (O(W) where W = write set size)
  - Production MAY group by shard to reduce lock acquisitions

## Commit Validation Algorithm (First-Committer-Wins)
- validate_commit(T, commit_index):
  - For each page in write_set: if commit_index.latest_commit_seq(pgno) > T.snapshot.high → conflict
  - On conflict: attempt algebraic merge (§5.10)
    - If merge possible: perform_merge
    - If not: return SQLITE_BUSY_SNAPSHOT (retryable)

## Serialized ↔ Concurrent Mode Interaction

### While Serialized-mode writer is Active (holding global write mutex):
- Concurrent txns MAY BEGIN and read normally
- Any Concurrent-mode page write lock attempt MUST fail with SQLITE_BUSY
  (allowing concurrent writers would violate SQLite single-writer contract)

### While any Concurrent-mode writer is Active (holds any page locks):
- Acquiring Serialized writer exclusion (BEGIN IMMEDIATE/EXCLUSIVE/DEFERRED upgrade) MUST fail with SQLITE_BUSY
- DEFERRED read-only begins remain permitted; only writer upgrade excluded

### Cross-Process Implementation
- SharedMemoryLayout maintains serialized_writer indicator (token + lease)
- Set when Serialized txn acquires writer exclusion, cleared at commit/abort
- Concurrent-mode write paths MUST check this indicator before acquiring page locks

### check_serialized_writer_exclusion(shm) Algorithm
- Load token (Acquire); if 0 → Ok
- Check expiry + process_alive(pid, birth)
- If alive and valid lease → SQLITE_BUSY
- If stale (lease expired or owner dead): CAS clear + retry loop
  - CAS failure means token changed (someone else cleared or new writer installed)
  - MUST retry to avoid returning Ok while new serialized writer is active

### Serialized Writer Acquisition Ordering (5 steps)
1. Acquire global serialized writer exclusion
2. Publish shared indicator (serialized_writer_token != 0, Release ordering)
3. Drain concurrent writers: wait until no outstanding page locks from Concurrent-mode txns
4. Perform writes
5. On commit/abort: CAS clear indicator + release global exclusion

### External Interop Hook (Compatibility mode)
- Concurrent-mode exclusion meaningless if legacy writer bypasses .fsqlite-shm
- Compatibility mode with .fsqlite-shm MUST exclude legacy writers (§5.6.6.1, §5.6.7)
- FORBIDDEN: multi-writer MVCC while legacy writers permitted

PARENT EPIC: bd-3t3
DEPENDS ON: bd-3t3.8 (SharedPageLockTable), bd-3t3.3 (Transaction Lifecycle), bd-3t3.1 (Core Types)

Dependencies:
  -> bd-3t3 (parent-child) - §5: MVCC Formal Model (Revised)
  -> bd-3t3.8 (blocks) - §5.6.3 SharedPageLockTable: Cross-Process Exclusive Locks
  -> bd-3t3.3 (blocks) - §5.4 Transaction Lifecycle (Begin/Read/Write/Commit/Abort)
  -> bd-3t3.1 (blocks) - §5.1 MVCC Core Types

Dependents:
  <- bd-1h3b (blocks) - §5.10.2-5.10.4 Deterministic Rebase + Physical Merge + Merge Policy
  <- bd-1onb (blocks) - §5.9.1-5.9.2 Write Coordinator Sequencers (Native + WAL Paths)
